description: Füllhorn Delbrück MVP — customer administration, eligibility checks and food distribution tracking. Converted from the 16 PRDs in tasks/, decomposed into layered vertical slices (domain → use case → Prisma → Next.js → Playwright) in dependency order. Non-negotiable rules: domain imports nothing from Next.js/React/Prisma, time comes from the Clock port, grown-up/children counts are derived never stored, money is integer cents, policy values are settings data with effectiveFrom, no actor is ever recorded (there is no login), Customer.id is a surrogate key and the 1..N customer number is a reusable slot.

userStories.title: Settings value objects and effective-from resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure module that resolves the policy values in force on a given date, so every other rule reads configuration through one seam. Source: tasks/prd-us-14-configure-business-rules.md §US-14.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/policy/settings.ts defines the settings shape: quotaN, portionsPerGrownUp, portionsPerChild, reminderThreshold, weekAnchor {isoWeek, colour}, distributionWeekday (ISO 1-7), priceTable as rows of {grownUps, children, cents}
resolveSettingsAt(versions, date) returns the version with the greatest effectiveFrom that is <= date
A date before the earliest version throws a typed NoSettingsInForce error, never a partial object
priceFor(settings, grownUps, children) returns the exact matching row's cents or throws a typed NoPriceForHousehold error naming both counts — it never interpolates
Construction validates quotaN >= 1, portion values >= 0, reminderThreshold >= 1, every cents value a non-negative integer
Tests cover: exact-boundary date, date between versions, date before all versions, missing price row
Module contains no I/O, no new Date() and no imports from Next.js, React or Prisma
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SettingsRepository port with readCurrentSettings and updateSettings use cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I need use cases that read the settings in force and append a new dated version, so the UI never touches persistence directly. Source: tasks/prd-us-14-configure-business-rules.md §US-14.2
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsRepository port added to src/application/ports.ts with listVersions() and append(version)
readCurrentSettings(deps) resolves versions against deps.clock.now()
updateSettings(deps, input) appends a new version and never mutates an existing one
updateSettings rejects with a typed QuotaBelowActiveCustomers error carrying both numbers when the new quotaN is below the current active customer count
updateSettings rejects a version whose effectiveFrom precedes the latest existing version
updateSettings writes an audit entry recording what changed, when and why, with no actor field
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SettingsVersion schema, seed and Prisma repository
userStories.description: As a developer, I need settings versions stored in SQLite and seeded with provisional defaults, so a fresh install boots into a working app. Source: tasks/prd-us-14-configure-business-rules.md §US-14.3
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma models SettingsVersion (id, effectiveFrom, scalar policy fields) and PriceTableRow (settingsVersionId, grownUps, children, cents)
Unique constraint on SettingsVersion.effectiveFrom and on (settingsVersionId, grownUps, children)
All money columns are Int cents — no Float or Decimal anywhere in schema.prisma

A seed routine inserts the provisional version (quotaN 240, 2 portions per grown-up, 1 per child, threshold 3, price 200c per grown-up + 100c per child, anchor 2026-W02 = RED, weekday Thursday) only when no version exists; running it twice is a no-op
PrismaSettingsRepository implements the port and is integration-tested against a throwaway SQLite file
The placeholder SchemaMarker model is removed
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Settings screen at /einstellungen
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to edit the quota, portions, price table, reminder threshold and week-cycle settings in the app, so FD can adapt without calling a developer. Source: tasks/prd-us-14-configure-business-rules.md §US-14.4
acceptanceCriteria: Route /einstellungen shows the currently-in-force values, each labelled in German
A Zod schema validates the form; euro input (e.g. 2,50) is converted to whole cents before leaving the adapter
The price table is edited as a grid of (grown-ups × children) rows; rows can be added and removed
Saving asks for an effective-from date, defaulting to today
Lowering quotaN below the active customer count shows a German error naming both numbers and saves nothing
Previous versions are listed read-only with their effective-from dates
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: edit a price and see it applied
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving the settings round-trip works against the built app. Source: tasks/prd-us-14-configure-business-rules.md §US-14.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec opens /einstellungen, changes the price for (1 grown-up, 0 children), saves effective today, reloads and asserts the new value is displayed
Spec asserts the quota-too-low path shows an error and leaves the stored value unchanged
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: HouseholdComposition — derive grown-up/children counts from birthdates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the grown-up/children split computed from birthdates against an injected clock, so counts can never drift from reality. Source: tasks/prd-us-01-register-customer.md §US-01.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/customer/householdComposition.ts exports composition(members, today) returning {grownUps, children}
A member counts as a grown-up on their 13th birthday; the day before they are a child
Tests cover the day before, the day of and the day after a 13th birthday
Test covers a 29 February birthdate evaluated in a non-leap year (grown-up from 1 March), with the convention documented in a comment
An empty member list throws a typed EmptyHousehold error, never {0, 0}
A future birthdate throws a typed BirthDateInFuture error
today is a parameter — no new Date() in the module
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CustomerNumber — lowest free slot allocation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure function that picks the lowest free customer number, so registration order is reproducible and testable. Source: tasks/prd-us-01-register-customer.md §US-01.2
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/customer/customerNumber.ts exports lowestFreeNumber(takenNumbers, quotaN)
Returns the smallest integer in 1..quotaN not present in takenNumbers
Returns a typed NoFreeCustomerNumber error when every number is taken
A gap left by an archived customer is filled before any higher number
Tests cover: empty list gives 1, [1,2,4] gives 3, full range gives the error, quotaN = 1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Group type and suggestGroup balancing rule
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want the app to suggest the smaller group, so Red and Blue stay roughly equal without me counting. Source: tasks/prd-us-01-register-customer.md §US-01.3
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/customer/group.ts exports the Group = 'RED' | 'BLUE' type and suggestGroup({red, blue})
Returns the group with fewer active customers
On a tie returns 'RED' deterministically — never random — and the choice is documented
Tests cover: red smaller, blue smaller, tie, both zero
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: registerCustomer use case
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one transactional use case that turns validated input into an active customer with a number, a group and a first card. Source: tasks/prd-us-01-register-customer.md §US-01.4
acceptanceCriteria: CustomerRepository port added to src/application/ports.ts with takenActiveNumbers(), groupCounts() and create(customer)

registerCustomer(deps, input) reads settings for quotaN, allocates the lowest free number, resolves the group (an explicit input choice wins over the suggestion) and persists the customer with status ACTIVE, reminderCount 0 and card index 1
Rejects with one typed error per case: NoFreeCustomerNumber, EmptyHousehold, BirthDateInFuture, MissingRequiredField
The whole operation is a single transaction — a failure leaves no partial customer and consumes no customer number
Writes an audit entry with no actor field
Test covers the race where the chosen number was taken between read and write: it must surface NoFreeCustomerNumber or retry, never create a duplicate
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Customer, HouseholdMember, Certificate and Card schema with the slot-vs-identity constraint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core customer tables with the reusable-slot rule enforced by the database, not just by application code. Source: tasks/prd-us-01-register-customer.md §US-01.5
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma models: Customer (surrogate id Int autoincrement, customerNumber, firstName, lastName, birthDate, street, houseNumber, zip, city, group, status, reminderCount, notes), HouseholdMember, Certificate (type, validUntil), Card (index, issuedAt)
No grownUps or children columns exist on Customer — the counts are derived, never stored
A partial/filtered unique index enforces at most one ACTIVE customer per customerNumber; archived rows are exempt
Every foreign key targets Customer.id, never customerNumber
Unique constraint on (customerId, index) for cards
PrismaCustomerRepository implements the port and is integration-tested against a throwaway SQLite file
Integration test proves two archived customers may both hold customer number 50
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Registration form at /kunden/neu
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want a registration screen that captures everything in one place and shows me the derived counts as I type. Source: tasks/prd-us-01-register-customer.md §US-01.6
acceptanceCriteria: Route /kunden/neu with a Zod-validated server action that calls registerCustomer and nothing else
Fields: first name, last name, date of birth, street, house number, ZIP, city, certificate type, certificate valid-until
Household members are repeatable rows (first name, last name, birthdate); the customer is pre-filled as the first member from the personal-data fields
Grown-up and children counts are shown as read-only derived values that update live — there is no input control for them
The proposed customer number and suggested group are shown; the group is changeable and both current group sizes are displayed
Each rejection renders its own German message: no free number, missing field, empty household, future birthdate
On success, redirect to the customer's card view
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

Spec asserts submitting with an empty household shows the German error and creates nothing

userStories.title: CardNumber value object
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a value object for card numbers, so 50k3 is never handled as an ad-hoc string. Source: tasks/prd-us-02-issue-customer-card.md §US-02.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/card/cardNumber.ts exports format(customerNumber, index), parse(text) and next(cardNumber)
format(50, 3) returns '50k3'; parse('50k3') returns {customerNumber: 50, index: 3}; next({50,3}) returns {50,4}
parse returns a typed InvalidCardNumber error for: '', '50', 'k3', '50k0', '50k', '050k3', '50k3x' and negative numbers
Input parsing accepts an uppercase K; output is always lowercase — documented in a comment
Index starts at 1, never 0
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: issueCard use case — the single card-issuing path
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one use case that issues the next card and invalidates the previous one, reused by first issue, loss and stale-count reissue. Source: tasks/prd-us-02-issue-customer-card.md §US-02.2
acceptanceCriteria: issueCard(deps, {customerId, reason}) creates a card with index = max existing index + 1, or 1 if none
Validity is derived from being the highest index — no separate mutable valid flag exists
Rejects with a typed error if the customer is archived
Writes an audit entry with reason FIRST_ISSUE | LOST | STALE_COUNTS | OTHER
Tested with fakes: first issue, second issue, archived customer, issue after a gap in indices (must take max+1)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Card repository with currentCard query
userStories.description: As a developer, I need card persistence that guarantees exactly one valid card per customer. Source: tasks/prd-us-02-issue-customer-card.md §US-02.3
acceptanceCriteria: Card model gains a reason field; @@unique([customerId, index]) is confirmed present
PrismaCardRepository exposes currentCard(customerId) returning the highest index, and issue(customerId, index, reason)
Integration test proves two different customers may both hold card number 50k1
Integration test proves a concurrent double-issue cannot create two cards with the same index — the unique constraint surfaces as a typed error

Tests pass

userStories.title: weekColour calendar alternation rule
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure function from a date to a week colour, so the Red/Blue alternation is testable without a database. Source: tasks/prd-us-03-week-colour.md §US-03.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/distribution/weekColour.ts exports colourOf(date, anchor) where anchor is {isoWeek, colour}
The colour flips on every ISO week boundary; parity is computed from the ISO week difference to the anchor
colourOf is total for dates before the anchor — tests cover one, two and fifty-three weeks before it
Property test asserts colourOf(d) !== colourOf(d + 7 days) for a swept range of dates
Tests cover ISO edge cases: the Sunday-to-Monday boundary, 1 January falling in week 52/53 of the prior ISO year, and a 53-week year
ISO-8601 week numbering (weeks start Monday) is used and documented in a comment
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: isDistributionDay and nextDistribution
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want the app to know whether today is a distribution day and, if not, when the next one is and what colour it will be. Source: tasks/prd-us-03-week-colour.md §US-03.2
acceptanceCriteria: isDistributionDay(date, weekday) compares against the configured ISO weekday
nextDistribution(date, settings) returns {date, colour} for the next distribution day at or after date
On a distribution day, nextDistribution returns today, not next week
Tests cover the day before, the day of and the day after a distribution day, and a configured weekday of Sunday (ISO 7)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: getWeekColour use case

Test proves a lookup for a past date uses the anchor that was in force then, not the current anchor

userStories.title: PortionPolicy — portions from counts and configured per-head values
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want the portion allowance derived automatically, so I do no arithmetic at a busy counter. Source: tasks/prd-us-07-portions-and-price.md §US-07.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/policy/portions.ts exports portionsFor({grownUps, children}, {portionsPerGrownUp, portionsPerChild})
Returns grownUps * portionsPerGrownUp + children * portionsPerChild as an integer
No default values are baked into the module — the configuration is a parameter
Tests cover: single-person household, household with children, zero children, portionsPerChild of 0

userStories.title: PricePolicy — exact price-table lookup in cents
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want the price read from the fixed table, so it never flexes with supply or occasion. Source: tasks/prd-us-07-portions-and-price.md §US-07.2
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/policy/price.ts exports priceFor({grownUps, children}, priceTable) returning integer cents
Looks up an exact (grownUps, children) row — it never interpolates, extrapolates or falls back to a nearest row
Returns a typed NoPriceForHousehold error carrying both counts when no row matches
Tests cover: exact match, no match, a valid zero-cent row
The module returns cents only; formatting goes through src/domain/money.ts
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: describeAllowance — counts, portions and price for a customer at a date
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one application helper that resolves settings, derives counts and returns the allowance, so the counter and record screens share it. Source: tasks/prd-us-07-portions-and-price.md §US-07.3
acceptanceCriteria: The helper returns {grownUps, children, portions, priceCents} for a household at a given date
A household with no matching price row surfaces NoPriceForHousehold rather than a partial result
Test proves a household evaluated on a date where an older settings version was in force is priced with that older version
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: evaluateAtCounter — the counter verdict rule
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure function returning exactly one verdict for a customer at the counter, so the decision is testable in milliseconds and cannot drift between screens. Source: tasks/prd-us-04-lookup-customer.md §US-04.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/distribution/counterVerdict.ts exports evaluateAtCounter(input) returning a discriminated union

Verdict cases are exactly: NOT_FOUND, ARCHIVED, BLOCKED (carries reason), WRONG_GROUP (carries customer group and today's colour), OUTDATED_CARD (carries presented and current card number), ALREADY_SERVED_TODAY, CLEAR_TO_SERVE, CLEAR_TO_SERVE_CERTIFICATE_EXPIRED (carries expiry date and reminder count)
Precedence is NOT_FOUND, ARCHIVED, BLOCKED, WRONG_GROUP, OUTDATED_CARD, certificate check, CLEAR_TO_SERVE — and every adjacent ordering pair has its own test
An expired certificate never produces a blocking verdict
today and weekColour are parameters — no clock and no I/O in the module
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: lookupCustomer use case
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a read-only use case that resolves a typed number to a customer and returns the verdict plus everything the counter screen shows. Source: tasks/prd-us-04-lookup-customer.md §US-04.2
acceptanceCriteria: Accepts a raw string and parses it as a card number (50k3) or a bare customer number (50)
A bare customer number resolves to the active holder of that slot; when only archived customers ever held it, the verdict is ARCHIVED for the most recent holder
A card index lower than the holder's current index yields OUTDATED_CARD; an unassigned customer number yields NOT_FOUND
The result carries name, customer number, group, counts, portions, priceCents, certificate expiry, status, reminder count, notes and current card number
Counts, portions and price are computed at read time — never read from stored columns
Test asserts the fake repository received no mutations for every verdict branch
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock for all verdict branches
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Counter lookup queries and indexes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the lookup to resolve reassigned slots correctly and stay instant. Source: tasks/prd-us-04-lookup-customer.md §US-04.3
acceptanceCriteria: findByCustomerNumber(n) returns the active holder, plus the most recent archived holder when there is no active one
Indexes exist on Customer.customerNumber and Customer.status
Household members, certificate and cards are loaded in one query — no N+1 at the counter
Integration test with an archived and an active customer both holding number 50 resolves correctly
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/ if the schema changed
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Counter lookup screen on /ausgabe
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to type a number and see one clear answer, so I can serve a queue quickly. Source: tasks/prd-us-04-lookup-customer.md §US-04.4

The verdict renders as a full-width banner with a distinct colour AND an explicit German sentence and icon — never colour alone

Wrong group renders the send-away sentence naming both colours; outdated card names the presented and the current card number; blocked shows the reason verbatim; archived states not eligible; expired certificate states serve-and-remind; otherwise clear to serve
Below the verdict: name, customer number, group, counts, portions, price, certificate expiry, status, reminder count and notes are all visible without further clicks
An unknown number renders 'Nummer nicht gefunden' and keeps the input focused
The UI switch over the verdict union is exhaustive with a never-check in the default branch
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Attendance rules — duplicate prevention and same-day correction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the attendance rules as pure functions, so duplicate prevention and same-day correction are testable without a database. Source: tasks/prd-us-05-record-attendance.md §US-05.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/distribution/attendance.ts exports canRecord(existingRecordsForCustomer, today) returning OK or a typed AlreadyServedToday error carrying the existing record's date
canCorrect(record, today) returns true only when record.date is the same calendar day as today
Day comparison is calendar-day based in Europe/Berlin and documented in a comment
Tests cover a midnight boundary and a DST change
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: recordAttendance and correctAttendance use cases
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want one action that records the hand-out, so the queue keeps moving. Source: tasks/prd-us-05-record-attendance.md §US-05.2
acceptanceCriteria: recordAttendance(deps, {customerId, paid}) writes one record with the customer's surrogate id, deps.clock.now(), showedUp true, the paid flag and priceCents resolved from the settings in force on that date
paid defaults to true and can be passed as false
A second invocation for the same customer on the same day rejects with AlreadyServedToday and writes nothing
The use case re-evaluates the counter verdict before writing and refuses ARCHIVED, BLOCKED and WRONG_GROUP — the UI is not the only guard
correctAttendance(deps, {recordId, ...}) amends or deletes a record made today; an older record rejects with a typed RecordNoLongerCorrectable error
Both write audit entries with no actor field
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock, including the same-day duplicate and next-day correction cases
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: DistributionRecord schema with the one-per-day constraint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need distribution records that survive indefinitely and cannot be duplicated per day. Source: tasks/prd-us-05-record-attendance.md §US-05.3
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma model DistributionRecord: id, customerId (FK to Customer.id), date, showedUp, paid, priceCents Int
A normalised date-only day-key column carries a unique constraint on (customerId, dayKey) so the database prevents a double record
Indexes on date and on (customerId, date)
No delete cascade from customer archiving — records outlive status changes
Integration test proves the duplicate constraint fires
A schema comment records that priceCents is deliberate redundancy alongside effective-from settings, so it is not 'cleaned up' later
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Serve action on the counter screen
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to confirm the hand-out with one keystroke without leaving the counter screen. Source: tasks/prd-us-05-record-attendance.md §US-05.4
acceptanceCriteria: A German 'Ausgabe erfassen' button appears on /ausgabe only when the verdict permits serving
A 'paid' checkbox is pre-checked and can be cleared before confirming
The button is keyboard-reachable and confirms with Enter; on success a short confirmation shows and the lookup input re-focuses
A second attempt shows 'Heute bereits versorgt' with the existing record's time
A correct-today's-entry control allows changing the paid flag or removing the record, with a confirmation step before removal

userStories.title: Certificate validity and reminder escalation rules
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the expiry and escalation rules as pure functions, so the grace period behaves identically everywhere. Source: tasks/prd-us-06-certificate-reminder.md §US-06.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/customer/certificate.ts exports isExpired(certificate, today)
A certificate is valid on its validUntil date and expired the day after — tested for the day before, the day of and the day after
escalation(reminderCount, threshold, expired) returns NONE, REMIND or PROMPT_ARCHIVE
PROMPT_ARCHIVE is returned only when the count has reached the threshold AND the certificate is still expired
A valid certificate always yields NONE regardless of the reminder count
today and threshold are parameters — no clock and no settings lookup in the module
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: recordReminder and renewCertificate use cases
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to log a reminder and record a renewed certificate, so the escalation is documented rather than remembered. Source: tasks/prd-us-06-certificate-reminder.md §US-06.2
acceptanceCriteria: recordReminder(deps, {customerId}) increments reminderCount by one and returns the new count
It rejects with a typed ReminderAlreadyLoggedToday error when a reminder exists for that customer on the same calendar day, and writes nothing
It rejects when the certificate is not expired
renewCertificate(deps, {customerId, type, validUntil}) records the new certificate and resets reminderCount to 0 in the same transaction
renewCertificate rejects a validUntil in the past with a typed error
Both write audit entries; the reminder entry records the resulting count
Tested against fakes and a fake clock: first reminder, same-day repeat, next-day reminder, reminder on a valid certificate, renewal resetting a count of 3
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Customer status transition state machine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need status transitions modelled as a state machine, so illegal transitions are impossible rather than merely unlikely. Source: tasks/prd-us-08-block-unblock-customer.md §US-08.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/customer/status.ts defines Status = 'ACTIVE' | 'BLOCKED' | 'ARCHIVED' and transition(from, to)

userStories.title: E2E: search and filter the customer list
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec covering search, filters and the group-balance figures. Source: tasks/prd-us-15-customer-list.md §US-15.4
acceptanceCriteria: Spec seeds customers across groups, statuses and certificate states
Spec asserts name search finds the right customer, a card number resolves to its holder, the blocked filter shows only blocked customers, and archived are hidden by default but appear when toggled
Spec asserts the group counts match the seeded data and do not change when a filter is applied
Spec asserts the URL carries the filter state and reloading restores the same view
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: updateHousehold use case
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to add and remove household members, so the record reflects reality as households change. Source: tasks/prd-us-16-maintain-customer-record.md §US-16.1
acceptanceCriteria: updateHousehold(deps, {customerId, members}) replaces the household member set
An empty member list rejects with EmptyHousehold and a future birthdate with BirthDateInFuture, reusing the registration domain rules rather than a second implementation
Derived counts, portions and price are never written
Test asserts a change that alters the counts puts the customer on the cards-due list with reason HOUSEHOLD_CHANGE
Test asserts a change that does not alter the counts (e.g. a spelling fix) does not put the customer on that list
Test asserts no history of the previous composition is kept
Writes an audit entry
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: updateCustomerDetails and updateNotes use cases
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to correct a customer's personal data, address and notes. Source: tasks/prd-us-16-maintain-customer-record.md §US-16.2 and §US-16.3
acceptanceCriteria: updateCustomerDetails updates name, date of birth and address with the same validation as registration

The customer's own record and their household-member entry stay consistent when their name or birthdate changes; the mechanism is decided, documented and tested (a single source of truth is preferred)
The customer number is not editable through this use case
updateNotes saves free text as given; empty is allowed, and a maximum length (4000 chars) is enforced with a clear message
Test asserts notes are returned by the counter lookup
Both write audit entries
Tested against hand-written fakes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: changeGroup use case
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to move a customer between Red and Blue, so the two groups stay roughly balanced. Source: tasks/prd-us-16-maintain-customer-record.md §US-16.4
acceptanceCriteria: changeGroup(deps, {customerId, group}) sets the group and returns the resulting group sizes
Changing to the same group rejects as a no-op
Test asserts a customer moved to Red is servable in a Red week on the same day
The change puts the customer on the cards-due list with reason GROUP_CHANGE, since the card prints the group
Writes an audit entry
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Customer record editing on /kunden/[id]
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want one screen where I can see and update everything about a customer. Source: tasks/prd-us-16-maintain-customer-record.md §US-16.5
acceptanceCriteria: Household members can be added and removed inline; counts, portions and price update live
The group control shows both current group sizes next to the choice
Notes are a multi-line field saved explicitly, with an indication that they appear at the counter
The certificate renewal form is embedded and shows the reminder count resetting to 0
Destructive actions (reissue card, block/unblock, archive) are grouped in a clearly separated section, each with its own confirmation
Distribution history is listed newest first with date, showed-up, paid and the price that applied
Each household member shows their current age next to their birthdate
An archived customer's record renders fully read-only
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: edit a household and see the effects
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving record edits propagate to counts, price, group and the counter. Source: tasks/prd-us-16-maintain-customer-record.md §US-16.6
acceptanceCriteria: Spec opens a customer record, adds a child, and asserts counts, portions and price update and that the customer appears on the cards-due list
Spec changes the group and asserts both group sizes update and the counter verdict for today flips accordingly
Spec edits the notes and asserts the text appears on the counter screen for that customer
Spec records a renewed certificate and asserts the reminder count shows 0
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
